channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Bipartisan lawmakers urge Biden to seek release of Americans detained in China
item.description: Two congressmen asked President Biden to further advocate for the release of wrongfully detained Americans in China in a letter Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israeli airstrikes rock southern suburbs of Beirut and cut off a key crossing into Syria
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel carried out another series of punishing airstrikes Friday, hitting suburban Beirut and cutting off the main border crossing between Lebanon and Syria for tens of thousands of people fleeing the Israeli bombardment of the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran's supreme leader makes rare speech after attacks on Israel
item.description: Iran's leader defended his country’s missile strikes on Israel this week, calling them “correct, logical, and lawful” in a rare public speech in Tehran on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: New MH370 project searches for answers on vanished plane
item.description: A new project is searching for answers about what happened to Malaysian Airlines Flight MH370 and its 239 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Biden: 'I don't know' if Netanyahu trying to influence election
item.description: President Biden on Friday said he wasn't aware of whether Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was trying to influence the U.S. election, while noting no administration has been a stronger ally to Israel than his own.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Boris Johnson alleges Netanyahu may have bugged his bathroom
item.description: Israel also was accused around the same time of planting listening devices in The White House, The Telegraph also reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: US launches airstrikes by fighter jets and ships on Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military struck more than a dozen Houthi targets in Yemen on Friday, going after weapons systems, bases and other equipment belonging to the Iranian-backed rebels, U.S. officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli military kills 250 Hezbollah terrorists since start of limited ground operation in Lebanon
item.description: Israel's military says it has killed 250 Hezbollah terrorists and has struck more than 2,000 military targets since it launched a limited ground operation in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sanxingdui Ruins were discovered accidentally by a farmer; has since been the site of over 60K relics
item.description: Sanxingdui Ruins is an archaeological site in Guanghan City. Official archaeological excavations have been conducted since 1934 at the site after its discovery in 1929.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico’s new president promises to resume fight against climate change
item.description: In her first days as Mexico’s new president, Claudia Sheinbaum made a point of distancing herself from the fossil fuel reliance promoted by her predecessor, Andres Manuel Lopez Obrador, and vowed to resume an energy transition that he halted.
item.dc:creator: Teresa De Miguel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court to weigh in on Mexico's lawsuit against US gun manufacturers
item.description: The Supreme Court agreed Friday to take up the gun industry’s appeal of a ruling allowing the Mexican government to proceed with its lawsuit against several prominent American firearm manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: Iran's Ayatollah Khamenei defends missile barrage against Israel in rare sermon
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei said during a rare public speech Friday that his country's missile attack on Israel this week was "legal and legitimate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Relatives say a whole family was killed in Israel's deadliest West Bank strike since Oct. 7
item.description: Relatives say an Israeli airstrike on a West Bank cafe that the military said targeted Palestinian militants also killed a family of four, including two young children.
item.dc:creator: Nasser Nasser And Aref Tufaha, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Boris Johnson: Netanyahu may have bugged my bathroom
item.description: Former United Kingdom Prime Minister Boris Johnson alleged in an interview with The Telegraph that his staff found a “listening device” in his personal bathroom after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had used it in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two London police officers reinstated over stop and search of Black athletes
item.description: Two former London police officers have been handed their jobs back and will receive back pay after winning an appeal against a ruling that said they had lied over a stop and search of two Black athletes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Clashes in Kenya as people discuss the deputy president's impeachment motion
item.description: Supporters and opponents of Kenya’s deputy president have clashed at public forums over an impeachment motion against him, which was introduced in parliament by the ruling alliance this week.
item.dc:creator: Evelyne Musambi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: An Israeli airstrike cuts a major highway linking Lebanon with Syria
item.description: An Israeli airstrike has cut off a main highway linking Lebanon with Syria, leaving two huge craters on either side of the road.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Democrats suspect Netanyahu attempting to tilt Trump-Harris race
item.description: Democrats increasingly suspect Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is trying to interfere in U.S. domestic politics by ignoring President Biden's calls to negotiate a peace deal in Gaza and by confronting Hezbollah and Iran weeks before the U.S. election.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Rainstorms and heavy floods hit large parts of Bosnia, killing at least 16 people
item.description: Severe rainstorms have struck Bosnia overnight, killing at least 16 people and flooding several towns and villages in central and southern parts of the country.
item.dc:creator: Eldar Emric, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Nobel Prizes will be announced against a backdrop of wars, famine and artificial intelligence
item.description: Wars, a refugee crisis, famine, and artificial intelligence could all be recognized with Nobel Prizes.
item.dc:creator: Mark Lewis, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Japan's new leader expresses regret for governing party slush fund scandal, vows stronger defense
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — New Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba opened his first policy speech in office Friday by expressing deep regrets over the governing party's slush funds scandal that dogged his predecessor.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump slams Biden for saying Israel shouldn't target Iranian nuclear sites: 'The craziest thing I've ever heard'
item.description: Former President Trump slammed President Biden for saying that Israel should not target Iranian nuclear sites, arguing the commander-in-chief's remark was "not the right answer." “I mean, that's the craziest thing I’ve ever heard. That's the biggest risk we have.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: New NATO chief Mark Rutte visits Ukraine in his first trip since taking office
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — New NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte visited Ukraine on Thursday in his first official trip since taking office and pledging the alliance's continued support for Kyiv in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two boats carrying migrants sink in the Red Sea off Djibouti's coast killing 45, UN says
item.description: DJIBOUTI (AP) — Two vessels carrying migrants from Africa sank in the Red Sea off the coast of Djibouti, killing 45 people, the U.N. migration agency said Tuesday. The boats had departed from Yemen carrying 310 people, the International Organization for Migration said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World War II-era bomb explodes at busy international airport, cratering taxiway
item.description: An American bomb from World War II buried beneath a Japanese airport exploded Wednesday, cratering a taxiway and forcing the cancellation of dozens of flights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WikiLeaks’ Julian Assange says he pleaded ‘guilty to journalism’ in order to be freed
item.description: In Julian Assange's first remarks since his release from prison in June, the WikiLeaks founder said he was freed after 5 years of incarceration because he 'pled guilty to journalism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 8 Israeli soldiers killed in Lebanon as Netanyahu says IDF engaged in 'tough war' with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel's military announced Wednesday that eight soldiers have died during "combat in southern Lebanon" as troops are wiping out Hezbollah infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ancient painting revealed in Egypt beneath layers of bird poop
item.description: During restoration of the Temple of Edfu, artwork was discovered beneath layers of bird poop, dust and soot covering the temple walls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Three young Swedes were arrested Wednesday in connection with two predawn explosions that occurred in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting a nearby Jewish school to close for the day. Police said no one was injured.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel, Iran swap threats as strikes on Lebanon continue
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has vowed to retaliate, saying Iran “made a big mistake" by launching a missile attack.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli military says regular infantry, armored units joining limited ground operation in southern Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military said regular infantry and armored units were joining what it has called its limited and localized ground operations in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran says it fired dozens of missiles into Israel, where residents are warned to shelter in place
item.description: Israel says Iran has fired missiles at Israel. Israel warned Lebanese people earlier Tuesday to evacuate border communities to avoid harm from limited ground operations it said were launched against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Iran missile barrage on Israel 'appears to have been defeated'
item.description: Israel telegraphed that an attack of this sort could result in retaliation against significant Iranian targets.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: WikiLeaks' Julian Assange says he pleaded 'guilty to journalism' in order to be freed
item.description: STRASBOURG, France (AP) — WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange said Tuesday that he was freed after years of incarceration because he "pled guilty to journalism.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL BASTIEN, BARBARA SURK and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A likely Russian artillery strike kills at least 6 at a Ukrainian market
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An apparent Russian artillery strike hit a market in the southern Ukraine city of Kherson on Tuesday, killing at least six people and wounding three others, authorities said, on a morning when Ukrainians across the country were observing a minute’s silence for their military and
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 148 are dead in floods and landslides in Nepal
item.description: KATHMANDU, Nepal (AP) — Rescuers in Nepal recovered dozens of bodies from buses and other vehicles that were buried in landslides near the capital Kathmandu, as the death toll from flooding rose to at least 148 with dozens missing, officials said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: BINAJ GURUBACHARYA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US airstrikes on Syria kill 37 militants affiliated with extremist groups
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Two U.S. airstrikes in Syria killed 37 militants affiliated with the Islamic State group and an al-Qaeda-linked group, the U.S. military said Sunday. It said two of the dead were senior militants. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
